label: LEMMA
claim: For distinct centers a and b in a selected-witness counterexample, the selected sets satisfy |S_a cap S_b| <= 2.
evidence: Two distinct Euclidean circles intersect in at most two points; see setup in the small-case summary.

label: LEMMA
claim: If S_i cap S_j = {a,b}, then the center chord p_i p_j is perpendicular to the witness chord p_a p_b.
evidence: Both centers lie on the perpendicular bisector of p_a p_b; see the small-case summary.

label: THEOREM
claim: There is no selected-witness counterexample with n=5.
evidence: For n=5 every selected 4-set is V\{i}, so two rows share three vertices, contradicting the two-circle cap.

label: THEOREM
claim: There is no selected-witness counterexample with n=6.
evidence: Writing S_i=V\{i,f(i)}, the rows S_i and S_f(i) share at least three vertices, contradicting the two-circle cap.

label: THEOREM
claim: There is no selected-witness counterexample with n=7.
evidence: The indegree and row-intersection counts saturate, the common-witness map is a permutation of 21 chords, and an odd cycle contradicts alternating perpendicularity.

label: LEMMA
claim: The incidence counting lower bound gives n >= 7 for any selected-witness counterexample.
evidence: The two-circle cap gives sum_j binom(d_j,2) <= 2 binom(n,2), while Jensen with average indegree 4 gives sum_j binom(d_j,2) >= 6n.

label: LEMMA
claim: For any unordered pair {a,b}, at most two vertices x of a strictly convex polygon can satisfy |x-a|=|x-b|.
evidence: The locus is the perpendicular bisector line of ab, and a line meets a strictly convex polygon boundary in at most two vertices.

label: LEMMA
claim: Any noncollinear triple of vertices can appear together in at most one selected witness set.
evidence: Three noncollinear points have a unique circumcenter, so at most one polygon vertex can center a circle through the triple.

label: LEMMA
claim: At a polygon vertex, the other vertices lie in the open convex cone between the two incident edges, so their boundary order agrees with angular order around that vertex.
evidence: Strict convexity makes every other vertex lie inside the interior angle at the hull vertex; the useful-findings digest records this as cone containment and angular order.

label: LEMMA
claim: For witnesses on one circle centered at a vertex, chord length is monotone in angular separation as long as the separation is below pi.
evidence: The chord formula |a-b|=2r sin(theta/2) is monotone on the open semicircle forced by the vertex cone.

label: LEMMA
claim: If all vertices of the polygon are cyclic, then no vertex has more than two other vertices at any one distance.
evidence: A circle centered at a vertex intersects the common circumcircle in at most two points.

label: LEMMA
claim: Four vertices are equidistant from p_i exactly when their paraboloid lifts are coplanar in a plane parallel to the tangent plane at the lift of p_i.
evidence: The handoff summary derives the standard z=x^2+y^2 lift for centered circles.

label: LEMMA
claim: The equation |p_i-p_a|^2=|p_i-p_b|^2 is affine-linear in the center coordinates p_i and quadratic overall.
evidence: The algebraic notes expand E_{i;a,b} and isolate its center-affine structure.

label: LEMMA
claim: If a,b,c are noncollinear and p_i is equidistant from them, then p_i is the circumcenter of triangle abc.
evidence: The algebraic notes state the circumcenter interpretation and use the fourth witness as the same-circle condition.

label: LEMMA
claim: The Jacobian of the selected squared-distance constraints always has the two translation directions and the rotation direction in its kernel.
evidence: The rank review states the Euclidean-motion kernel lemma for F_{i,a,b}.

label: LEMMA
claim: At any exact solution of the selected squared-distance equations, the scaling direction is also in the Jacobian kernel, so rank R_W(p) <= 2n-4 for a nondegenerate solution.
evidence: Homogeneity gives R_W(p)p=2F_W(p), hence R_W(p)p=0 on the solution set.

label: LEMMA
claim: A witness set from a hull vertex may lie in a semicircle of its witness circle, so the center need not lie in the convex hull of the witnesses.
evidence: The algebraic correction gives the semicircle criterion and a pentagon example.

label: CONJECTURE
claim: Every realizable k=4 counterexample may admit an ear-orderable selected witness pattern.
evidence: This is Bridge Lemma A prime / Key Peeling Lemma in the rank-and-bridge summary; it remains the central open bottleneck.

label: CONJECTURE
claim: The Lemma 12 endpoint-descent route would work if the endpoint-control auxiliary claim held for at least one endpoint of a minimal bad circle.
evidence: The synthesis isolates the outside-of-A endpoint-control claim as the missing global statement.

label: CONJECTURE
claim: Erdos #97 would follow if every bad vertex forced a 3-critical 4-tie and strictly convex n-gons had fewer than n such ties.
evidence: The corrected global-counting framework replaces the false quadruple count with bridge and critical-counting conjectures.

label: CONJECTURE
claim: A canonical short-base chord rule might assign distinct noncrossing chords to bad vertices, but injectivity is unproved.
evidence: The synthesis records the noncrossing-diagonal route and its canonical-chord injectivity gap.

label: CONJECTURE
claim: The three-support smallest-enclosing-circle case may close if a three-cap bridge lemma forces two witnesses of a bad support vertex into the opposite cap.
evidence: The synthesis records the diameter case as settled and the three-cap case as open.

label: INCIDENCE_PATTERN
claim: B12_3x4_danzer_lift is the leading saved block pattern but currently degenerates toward three tight clusters.
evidence: Repo handoff and current state list its formula and diagnostics as a near-miss degeneration.

label: INCIDENCE_PATTERN
claim: B20_4x5_FR_lift is the next block/symmetric pattern to test with a margin sweep and anti-clustering diagnostics.
evidence: The repo handoff preserves B20 as the second-ranked candidate pattern.

label: INCIDENCE_PATTERN
claim: P18_parity_balanced and P24_parity_balanced are period-2 incidence patterns worth search testing.
evidence: The repo handoff preserves both parity-balanced patterns in the ranked candidate list.

label: INCIDENCE_PATTERN
claim: C17_skew, C19_skew, and palindromic circulants remain search patterns only and must not be paired with forced coordinate symmetry.
evidence: The repo handoff candidate list and failed-idea section warn that incidence symmetry is not coordinate symmetry.

label: INCIDENCE_PATTERN
claim: The n=8 cube witness pattern is unrealizable by an orthocenter obstruction, but this does not settle all n=8 witness patterns.
evidence: The synthesis separates the real cube-pattern obstruction from the unproved uniqueness claim for n=8.

label: INCIDENCE_PATTERN
claim: The n=39 circulant pattern S_i={i+18,i-18,i+19,i-19} mod 39 is impossible because adjacent rows share three targets.
evidence: Modulo 39 this is {i+18,i+19,i+20,i+21}, so S_i cap S_{i+1} has size 3 and violates the two-circle cap.

label: INCIDENCE_PATTERN
claim: Prime cyclic and Sidon-type four-offset families with all row overlaps <=2 are plausible future incidence sources.
evidence: The n=39 degeneracy note proposes examples such as n=41 with offsets {5,14,24,34} and n=43 with {6,15,27,36}.

label: NUMERICAL_EVIDENCE
claim: The saved B12 run has max squared-distance spread about 0.006806368780585714 and convexity margin about 1e-6, so it is a near-miss only.
evidence: Existing data/runs/best_B12_slsqp_m1e-6.json plus the repo handoff diagnostics; not routed to claims.md as proof.

label: NUMERICAL_EVIDENCE
claim: The archive C12 offsets {4,5,8,11} artifact has max spread about 1.85e-6 but fails the required 1e-6 verification threshold.
evidence: Normalized artifact data/runs/archive_C12_offsets_4_5_8_11_near_convex.json from the machine-readable archive.

label: NUMERICAL_EVIDENCE
claim: The archive C12 offsets {2,3,4,10} artifact has near-zero equality residual only because the configuration collapses and is not strictly convex.
evidence: Normalized artifact data/runs/archive_C12_offsets_2_3_4_10_degenerate.json from the machine-readable archive.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: The claim that n=8 is proved by reducing to the cube witness pattern is not justified.
evidence: The cube pattern is ruled out, but the source does not prove it is the only n=8 witness pattern.
failure_mode: Unproved incidence uniqueness: n=8 has counting slack, so double regularity and cube uniqueness do not follow from the n=7 saturation argument.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: The claim that direct counting proves n>=13 or rules out n<=12 is wrong.
evidence: The corrected count has a missing factor of two and yields only n>=7.
failure_mode: Incorrect global count: the upper bound is 2*binom(n,2), not binom(n,2), so the claimed n>=13 threshold collapses to n>=7.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: A generic Jacobian rank of 2n-3 at non-solutions does not prove nonexistence of counterexamples.
evidence: Actual solutions lie on the constraint variety where scaling adds a kernel direction and rank can drop.
failure_mode: Rank sampled off the solution variety misses the homogeneous scaling kernel present at F=0.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: The circumcenter of a cyclic convex quadrilateral need not lie inside the quadrilateral.
evidence: The algebraic correction gives witness points in a semicircle around a hull vertex.
failure_mode: Semicircle obstruction: four equidistant witnesses from a hull vertex can lie in a small arc, placing the center outside their convex hull.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: The middle-neighbor forest lemma is false.
evidence: The forest review gives an affine regular 24-gon construction producing cycles in the middle-neighbor graph.
failure_mode: Structured cycle in the proposed middle-neighbor graph; cap regions can overlap or be incomparable instead of forming a forest.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: Forced indegree-four regularity is valid at n=7 saturation but is not available for general n.
evidence: The synthesis notes that Jensen is tight only at n=7 and there is slack for n>=8.
failure_mode: Saturation failure: the equality conditions used to force all indegrees to 4 disappear once n>=8.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: Using a weak witness definition based on separate repeated-distance pairs is not equivalent to four witnesses on one circle.
evidence: The synthesis warns that superseded notes used the weak definition and reached invalid conclusions.
failure_mode: Definition mismatch: pairwise repeated distances do not produce one selected 4-set at a common radius.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: The n=39 circulant optimization branch should be abandoned because the fixed incidence pattern violates the row-overlap cap exactly.
evidence: Adjacent rows share three targets before geometry enters, so the numerical collapse was diagnosing an impossible pattern.
failure_mode: Exact incidence obstruction: S_i and S_{i+1} have a three-point intersection, impossible for two distinct witness circles.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: Assuming equal-distance witnesses form one consecutive cyclic block around the polygon is geometrically false.
evidence: The rank-and-bridge summary lists this as a failed bridge-lemma step.
failure_mode: Cyclic polygon order around the boundary is not enough to force repeated-distance classes to be one consecutive boundary interval.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: Counting all centered concyclic quadruples is not a stable route to the problem.
evidence: One vertex can have Theta(n) other vertices on one circle centered there, creating Theta(n^4) ordered quadruples.
failure_mode: Artificial multiplicity blow-up: one high-multiplicity center dominates the count without global obstruction.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: Imposing one global common radius solves only a stricter subcase and should not be used as the full problem.
evidence: The synthesis says the uniform-radius subcase is distinct and the variable-radius case is the content of Erdos #97.
failure_mode: Overrestricted model: bidirectional/common-radius arguments stop applying once radii may vary by center.

label: FAILED_APPROACH
claim: Treating the B12 near-miss as evidence for a counterexample is unsafe.
evidence: The repo handoff says the residual improves as the configuration collapses toward three clusters.
failure_mode: Boundary degeneration: decreasing convexity or edge margins improves residual, pointing to a clustered limit rather than a strict solution.

label: EXACTIFICATION
claim: A certificate must encode exact coordinates or algebraic parameters, selected sets S_i, exact zero checks for distance equations, and exact positivity checks for convexity.
evidence: The repo handoff and exactification requirements list the certificate data and polynomial identities.

label: EXACTIFICATION
claim: Strict convexity in a fixed cyclic order should be encoded by orientation inequalities against each edge line, not only consecutive triples in unconstrained coordinate space.
evidence: The algebraic correction warns that consecutive left turns alone do not always rule out global self-intersection or wrong cyclic order.

label: EXACTIFICATION
claim: Fixed-pattern exactification should study special rank-drop loci compatible with the scaling kernel rather than generic full-rank samples.
evidence: The rank review recommends classifying rank-deficient solution loci after the scaling-kernel correction.

label: SAT_SMT
claim: The mandatory Boolean incidence constraints are x[i,i]=0, row sum 4, and pairwise selected-row overlap at most 2.
evidence: The repo handoff SAT/SMT section gives the base abstraction and explains what SAT and UNSAT mean.

label: SAT_SMT
claim: SAT/SMT filters may add pair/triple sharing, perpendicularity templates, or order-type constraints only after they are proved necessary.
evidence: The current SAT/SMT plan and computational summaries warn that heuristic balance or symmetry filters only rule out restricted subclasses.

label: LITERATURE_RISK
claim: Danzer and Fishburn--Reeds are k=3 nearby examples and do not solve or refute the k=4 target.
evidence: The useful-findings digest corrects Danzer attribution and records Fishburn--Reeds as a 20-point k=3 unit-distance example.

label: LITERATURE_RISK
claim: Before any solution claim, the public Erdos Problems listing and related formal-conjectures records need a fresh status check.
evidence: The repo handoff lists this as baseline literature hygiene for a public ledger.

label: LITERATURE_RISK
claim: The uniform-radius subcase depends on convex unit-distance literature and should be cited separately from the variable-radius problem.
evidence: The synthesis cites Edelsbrunner-Hajnal, Furedi, and later convex unit-distance bounds as background for only the common-radius subcase.

label: HEURISTIC
claim: Run the row-overlap filter |S_i cap S_j|<=2 before optimizing any fixed incidence pattern.
evidence: The computational summary provides a row_overlap_filter snippet and treats any violation as an exact obstruction.

label: HEURISTIC
claim: Search should optimize per-vertex quartets and per-vertex radii directly before freezing stabilized quartets for exact solving.
evidence: The computational summary says global-distance upgrades are overrestrictive and recommends the real per-vertex objective.

label: HEURISTIC
claim: Numerical candidates should be judged by residuals relative to nondegeneracy scales such as minimum pair distance, minimum edge length, and convexity margin.
evidence: The computational summary lists these diagnostics as required acceptance standards.
